originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Keeping Everyone Safe: News from Chad and Sudan
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, bringing you important news in a way that's easy to understand. We're going to talk about two countries that are neighbors, just like your house might be next to your friend's house. These countries are Chad and Sudan.

Our Neighbor Countries: Chad and Sudan

Chad and Sudan are two big countries in Africa. They share a very long line between them, called a border. Imagine it like an invisible line on the ground. On one side is Chad, and on the other side is Sudan. People in both countries are often very friendly and kind, just like you are with your neighbors. Did you know? Chad is sometimes called “The Land of Living Arts” because its people love music, dance, and storytelling! They have amazing cultures.

Something Sad Happened

Sometimes, even good neighbors can have problems. In Sudan, there has been some trouble, like grown-ups having a big disagreement. Sadly, something very sad happened near the border. Some people in Chad were gathered together because they were very, very sad. Someone they loved had passed away. When people gather like this to be sad together, we call them "mourners." They were feeling very quiet and sad, remembering their loved one. Then, a flying machine, which is sometimes called a drone, came from the sky. It dropped something bad that hurt many people. Seventeen people were hurt very badly, and they died. This made everyone in Chad very, very sad and worried. It's like if something bad happened right next to your house, and it made you feel unsafe.

Chad's Leader Wants to Keep Everyone Safe

When something like this happens, the leaders of a country need to make sure their people are safe. The leader of Chad is President Idris Mahamat Déby. He is like the head of the big family that is Chad. When he heard about the sad event, he felt very concerned and wanted to act fast. President Déby said that Chad must make sure its people are protected. He doesn't want bad things from Sudan to cross the border and hurt more people in Chad. He wants all the children, mothers, fathers, and grandmas and grandpas in Chad to be safe and sound. He cares a lot about his country's people. Did you know? Presidents are like super-important leaders who help make big decisions for their whole country! They work very hard.

Closing the Big Door

To keep people safe, President Déby made a big decision. He said that the border between Chad and Sudan must be closed. Imagine if you have a gate or a door to your house, and there's a storm outside. You would close the door to keep everyone inside safe, right? That's what Chad did. Closing the border means that for now, people and things cannot easily go from Chad to Sudan, or from Sudan to Chad, along that line. It's like putting a big "stop" sign up. This is to make sure that the problems happening in Sudan don't accidentally come into Chad and cause more sadness or trouble. It's a temporary action to protect everyone.

Soldiers Are Watching Carefully

President Déby also asked the soldiers in Chad to be extra careful. Soldiers are like guardians who help protect a country. They wear special uniforms and have important jobs. He told them to be on "high alert." This means they need to watch very, very closely near the border, all the time. They are watching to make sure no more bad flying machines come, and no bad trouble tries to sneak into Chad. They are like very brave helpers who stand guard to keep everyone inside the country safe. It's a bit like when your parents tell you to be extra careful if there's something tricky going on outside. Did you know? Soldiers also help during floods or other natural problems to keep people safe! They are not just about fighting; they help people in many ways.

Why Is There Trouble in Sudan?

You might be wondering, "Why are there problems in Sudan?" Sadly, in Sudan, some grown-ups are having a very big fight. It's a conflict, which is a grown-up word for a war. This fight has been going on for a little while, and it makes many people in Sudan very sad and scared too. They also want peace. Chad's leader and people wish for the fighting in Sudan to stop. When neighbors have problems, it's always better if they can talk and find a way to be peaceful again. Chad wants Sudan to be peaceful so that everyone can live happily and safely in both countries. Did you know? Sudan is famous for its ancient pyramids, just like Egypt! They are very old and tell stories of long, long ago when great kings and queens lived there.

Hoping for Peace and Friendship

Even though this is a sad story, there is always hope. Leaders like President Déby are working hard to protect their people and to hope for peace. When countries are neighbors, it's always best if they can be friends and help each other. We hope that the leaders of Chad and Sudan can talk to each other. Talking is often the best way to solve problems, just like when you talk to your friends when you have a disagreement. We hope that soon, the border can be open again, and people can travel safely between these two amazing African countries. We wish for peace and safety for everyone in Chad and Sudan. Thank you for listening, little news explorers! Remember to always be kind to your neighbors.
summary: Chad's leader closed the border and told soldiers to watch carefully after a flying machine from Sudan hurt people in Chad, because he wants to keep everyone safe and hopes for peace between the two neighbor countries.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how leaders work hard to keep their people safe when problems happen nearby, and it reminds us that peace and friendship between neighbor countries are very important for everyone to live happily and without fear.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Oh No! Senegal's Big Football Party Has to Move!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends and fellow football fans across Africa! Your kid-friendly reporter is here with some news from the world of football. Sometimes things happen that make us a little sad, and today we have a story about a surprise that happened to a country called Senegal.

Let's Talk About Football!

Imagine your favourite game is football. Now imagine a really, really big football party where all the best teams from different countries in Africa come to play! That's what the Africa Cup of Nations, or AFCON for short, is all about. It's a super exciting tournament that happens every two years, and everyone loves to watch it. *Did you know?* The first AFCON happened way back in 1957! That's even before your parents were born! It has been bringing joy and excitement to African countries for many, many years.

Senegal Was Going to Host a Big Party!

Last year, there was big happy news for the country of Senegal! The people who organise AFCON, a group called CAF (it stands for Confederation of African Football), chose Senegal to be the host country for the AFCON tournament in 2025. This was like getting the biggest, most exciting present ever! Being a host country means you get to welcome all the football teams and their fans. You get to build beautiful new stadiums, make sure there are nice places for everyone to stay, and get ready for lots of visitors. It's a chance to show the whole world how wonderful your country is, with its friendly people, amazing food, and beautiful places. The people in Senegal were so excited! They started dreaming about how wonderful AFCON 2025 would be in their country. They imagined colourful flags everywhere, lots of music, and everyone cheering for their favourite teams right in Senegal.

Oh No! The Party is Moving!

But then, something surprising happened. Just recently, CAF made a new decision. They said that Senegal will *not* be hosting the AFCON games in 2025 anymore. It was a very unexpected announcement. Imagine planning a huge birthday party, sending out all the invitations, and then suddenly, someone says the party has to move to a different house! That's how it felt for many people in Senegal. This news has made many people in Senegal feel very sad and disappointed. They had big plans and big hopes for AFCON 2025. It's a bit like having a wonderful dream, and then waking up to find it's not going to come true.

Why Is This Sad for Senegal?

Hosting AFCON is not just about playing football. It's about much more! When a country hosts a big event like this, many good things can happen. Lots of visitors come, and they spend money in shops, restaurants, and hotels, which helps the country's businesses. It also helps to build new things, like better roads and bigger airports. More importantly, hosting AFCON makes the people of that country feel incredibly proud! It's a chance to show off their culture, their talent, and their hospitality to the whole world. So, losing the chance to host is a big disappointment because all those dreams and opportunities are now gone for 2025.

A Friend From Senegal Speaks Out

There is a very important man named Mr. Augustin Senghor. He is part of the big CAF group, and he is also from Senegal. When he heard the news that Senegal would not host AFCON 2025, he was very upset. He said that this decision was "unacceptable." That's a strong word, and it means he thinks it's not fair and not right at all. He felt that his country was treated badly and that the decision should be spoken out against. He wants to make sure that everyone understands how sad and unfair this feels to the people of Senegal. He really cares about his country and about African football.

What Happens Now?

So, if Senegal isn't hosting, who will? Well, CAF now needs to find another country to host the big AFCON party in 2025. We don't know yet which country they will choose, but it will be another wonderful African nation that loves football just as much. The football party will still happen, just in a different place. It's always exciting to see which country will be picked next! *Did you know?* Africa is a very big continent with 54 different countries, and almost all of them absolutely love football! Many countries would be happy to host the AFCON.

Keep Playing!

Even when sad or surprising things happen, it's important to remember that football is about having fun, working together as a team, and always trying your very best. Senegal might be feeling sad today, but they have amazing football players, and they will keep playing wonderful football. Maybe one day, they will host an even bigger and better party! We send our best wishes to all our friends in Senegal. Let's keep our spirits high and remember the joy that football brings to all of us across Africa!
summary: Senegal will no longer host the big Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) football tournament in 2025, a decision that made many people, especially a senior football official from Senegal named Mr. Augustin Senghor, feel very sad and upset.
whyMatters: This news is important because hosting AFCON is a huge honor for a country, bringing lots of visitors, new buildings, and a chance to show off to the world, so losing this opportunity is a big disappointment for Senegal and its football fans.

originalTitle: DR Congo and Rwanda agree to ease tensions after talks in US
title: Happy News: DR Congo and Rwanda Are Talking!
content:
Hello, Friends! Big News from Our Home, Africa!

Hello, my super friends! It’s me, your friendly news reporter for Africa. I have some really good news to share with you today. It's about two countries in our amazing continent, Africa. They are called DR Congo and Rwanda. Guess what? They have decided to talk to each other to become better friends! Isn't that wonderful? When friends talk, they can solve any problem. This is very happy news for everyone.

Two Countries Who Are Neighbors

DR Congo and Rwanda are like two houses next door to each other. They share a border, which means they are very close. It's like your house and your friend's house on the same street! DR Congo is a very big country. It has huge green forests and lots of amazing animals. Rwanda is a smaller country, but it is very beautiful with many green hills. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the whole world! It has so many different countries and cultures.

What Was the Problem?

Sometimes, even good neighbors can have little disagreements. It’s just like when you and your friend might argue about a toy. DR Congo and Rwanda had some "tensions." "Tensions" is a grown-up word. It means they were not feeling very friendly towards each other. They had some problems that made them a little bit sad and worried. This is called a "conflict." A conflict means people or countries are not getting along. It can make things difficult for everyone living there. Did you know? Talking about your feelings can help you solve problems with your friends! It's always good to share what's on your mind.

Talking Helps Us Solve Problems!

The grown-ups in charge of DR Congo and Rwanda decided that talking was the best way to make things better. And they are right! Talking is super important. Imagine if you and your friend stopped talking when you had a problem. It would be hard to be friends again, right? But if you sit down and chat, you can understand each other. These countries had a special meeting. It was like a very important chat between their leaders. They wanted to "ease tensions." "Ease tensions" means to make the problems feel less big and scary. It means to start feeling friendly again and help each other.

Meeting Far Away in the USA

This special chat didn't happen in Africa this time. It happened very far away! It was in a country called the United States of America, or USA for short. It's good to sometimes talk in a different place. It can help everyone feel calm and fair. They went there so they could focus on making things better without any distractions. Did you know? The United States of America is very far from Africa, across a very big ocean! It's a long journey, but it was worth it for peace.

What Did They Promise?

A little while ago, in December, these two countries made a special promise called a "peace deal." A peace deal is like a big pinky promise that says: "We will try our best to be friendly and not fight." But sometimes, even after a promise, things can still be a bit tricky. It's like when you promise to share your toys, but then you forget for a little while. So, this new meeting was like saying, "Let's remember our promise! Let's try even harder to be good neighbors." They agreed to work together again. They agreed to talk more often and to help each other. This is like when friends agree to play together and share their snacks. It makes everyone happy. Did you know? When people make peace, it means they choose friendship over fighting. It makes the world a happier place for everyone.

Why Is This Good News for Everyone?

When countries are friends, many good things happen. Children can go to school safely. Grown-ups can work without worry. Everyone can play and laugh more! Peace means that families don't have to be scared. It means that food can travel easily between countries. It means people can visit their friends and family across the border. When there is peace, people can build new things, like hospitals and schools. They can plant more food and help beautiful animals live safely in their homes. This good news means that DR Congo and Rwanda are trying their best to make life better for all their people, especially for children like you. Did you know? In Africa, there are many amazing animals like elephants, giraffes, and zebras! Peace helps to protect their homes too.

What Can We Learn from This?

This story teaches us something very important. It teaches us that even when there are problems, talking can make things better. Always try to talk things out with your friends and family. It shows us that being kind and trying to understand each other is a very powerful thing. When you listen to someone, you can find a way to be friends again. Sharing and helping each other makes our world a brighter place. Just like these two countries are learning to share and help each other. Did you know? Sharing a smile with someone can make their day much better! Try it today!

Let's All Be Friends!

So, let's cheer for DR Congo and Rwanda! They are showing us how to be good neighbors and good friends. This makes our whole African family stronger and happier. Let's hope that they keep talking and keep building a wonderful friendship. Because when everyone is friends, we can all live in peace and harmony. We are so proud of our African family for choosing to talk and choosing peace. Hooray for DR Congo and Rwanda! Hooray for friendship!
summary: DR Congo and Rwanda, two neighbor countries in Africa, have agreed to talk and work together to be friends again after some disagreements.
whyMatters: When countries are friends, everyone is safer and happier, allowing children to play and learn, and families to live peacefully.
